_notes.source: CalRecycle new residential wood-frame construction rule of thumb

Asphalt & Concrete.notes: Only if site has existing AC paving being demolished - not applicable here

Brick / Masonry / Tile.notes: Not applicable - new construction, no masonry demo

Cabinets, Doors, Fixtures, Windows.notes: New install - packaging waste captured under Cardboard

Cardboard.notes: Packaging from windows, fixtures, appliances, materials

Drywall.notes: Interior walls + ceilings, 2 floors; cutoff waste ~10-15% of installed area

Landscape Debris.notes: Site clearing; minimal for infill ADU

Mixed C&D Debris.notes: Residual mixed waste from framing; 50% diverted via certified C&D facility

Roofing Materials.notes: New install packaging + underlayment waste; comp shingle offcuts

Scrap Metal.notes: Framing connectors, hangers, conduit offcuts, plumbing

Stucco.notes: 2-story = ~2,500+ SF exterior wall area; application waste ~10-15%; significant category

Unpainted Wood & Pallets.notes: Framing cutoffs; largest single category for wood-frame; pallets from delivery

Garbage / Trash.notes: General site trash; no recycle credit; goes to landfill

Other.notes: Leave blank unless specific material identified
